steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes how the reasons for linking Unamuno to the Generation of '98 may seem. The word “but” signals a contrast: whatever the reasons seem like at first, the author warns they risk overlooking subtleties. The blank must fit a word that makes the reasons sound convincing or valid — so that “but” introduces a genuine counter-argument.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'incongruous': means out of place or inconsistent. If the reasons already seem incongruous, the “but” warning loses force — there's no contrast. ✗
  • Choice B — 'pretentious': means showy or self-important. This casts the reasons in a negative light before the “but,” so no real contrast follows. ✗
  • Choice C — 'provocative': means controversial or stimulating debate. This makes the reasons sound questionable from the start, not compelling, so “but” doesn't create the needed turn. ✗
  • Choice D — 'irrefutable': means impossible to deny. If the reasons seem undeniably solid, the “but” clause creates a meaningful contrast — they look airtight, yet they still risk missing subtleties in Unamuno's style. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
